jokes.text: It's a fiber shake. I've got an interview with an old Princeton chum of Dad's. I need to be strong. I need to be alert. And I need to be regular.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains his health drink with serious academic preparation
jokes.punchline: The unexpected escalation to needing to be 'regular' (bathroom habits)
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton character moment - his earnest over-preparation combined with TMI about bodily functions. Well-structured with good escalation.

jokes.text: Ain't nothing in the world gonna make you regular, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton's earnest declaration about needing to be regular
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment that Carlton is fundamentally irregular
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will comeback - economical and character-specific. Takes Carlton's literal meaning and twists it.

jokes.text: You better let that man eat and give him food... or he'll mess around and chew one of your arms off in the car.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv suggesting Uncle Phil doesn't need to pack food for SeaWorld
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated warning about Uncle Phil's eating habits turning violent
jokes.explanation: Great character-based humor about Uncle Phil's legendary appetite. The escalation to cannibalism is absurd but earned.

jokes.text: Now, why you go and do that? You certainly ain't big, and as far as being a brother...
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions becoming a 'Big Brother' volunteer
jokes.punchline: Will's literal interpretation pointing out Carlton is neither big nor Black
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - literal size, racial identity, and Carlton's disconnect from his heritage. Sharp and economical.

jokes.text: Don't worry, Carlton, it'll shrink.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentions a dryer mishap with Carlton's sweater
jokes.punchline: Will's deadpan 'comfort' that implies Carlton himself will shrink to fit
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and misdirection. The audience expects comfort but gets a height joke instead.

jokes.text: Well, you tell those little brats I don't like them either.
jokes.setup: Carlton being rejected as a Big Brother volunteer
jokes.punchline: His petulant response to children not liking him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - his inability to handle rejection leads to childish behavior with actual children.

jokes.text: Uh, that depends, are you going as Archie or Jughead?
jokes.setup: Carlton asking if his sweater will work for his interview
jokes.punchline: Will comparing him to Archie Comics characters based on his preppy style
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference that captures Carlton's wholesomely nerdy aesthetic. Works well for the era.

jokes.text: Dole headquarters? No, man, we don't want no pineapples.
jokes.setup: Carlton expecting a call from his volunteer job application
jokes.punchline: Will answering and treating it like a fruit vendor call
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on Will's street-smart assumption versus Carlton's corporate aspirations.

jokes.text: Mama, no!
jokes.setup: Carlton looking at his grades and being confident about his academics
jokes.punchline: His horrified reaction seeing his actual grades
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat. The 'Mama, no!' is a classic exclamation of despair.

jokes.text: Hey, what's up, G? Another Village People reunion?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey appearing in cowboy attire
jokes.punchline: Will's reference to the disco group known for costumes
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but somewhat predictable for cowboy outfit mockery.

jokes.text: There's nothing like the feel of an animal beneath you to recharge your battery.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey explaining his dude ranch weekend
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally suggestive phrasing about horseback riding
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for innuendo humor. Geoffrey's innocent delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: I know what you're talking about there, G. No, I'm afraid you don't, Master William. But I do find it rather endearing when you think you do.
jokes.setup: Will thinking he caught Geoffrey's innuendo
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sophisticated put-down maintaining his innocence while mocking Will
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Geoffrey moment. His condescending yet affectionate dismissal is perfectly in character and expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Has the world gone mad?
jokes.setup: Carlton examining different colored M&Ms
jokes.punchline: His existential crisis over blue M&Ms existing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton absurdity. His over-reaction to a minor change captures his rigid worldview and resistance to change.

jokes.text: They're just M&M's, C.
jokes.setup: Carlton's existential crisis over blue M&Ms
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact reality check
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Will's grounded perspective contrasts Carlton's melodrama effectively.

jokes.text: I haven't seen him since high school. Seeing him would cheer me up. I mean, he was always such a loser. He was even shorter than me.
jokes.setup: Will calling Stinky McGuire to cheer up Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton's mean-spirited anticipation of feeling superior to someone 'even shorter'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the visual reveal of Stinky being tall now. Carlton's petty joy about height superiority is very in character.

jokes.text: [Visual gag: Stinky enters - now tall and attractive]
jokes.setup: Carlton expects to see his short, loser friend Stinky
jokes.punchline: Stinky is now tall, handsome, and successful
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual subversion of expectations. Carlton's backup plan backfires spectacularly.

jokes.text: My sweater shrunk. My grades are low. My head is too small for my body.
jokes.setup: Will mocking Carlton's complaints
jokes.punchline: The escalation from real problems to absurd body dysmorphia
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with callback to Will's earlier shrinking joke. The body dysmorphia line is unexpectedly specific and funny.

jokes.text: It is?
jokes.setup: Will sarcastically listing Carlton's complaints including head size
jokes.punchline: Carlton's genuine concern about his head-to-body ratio
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his vanity and insecurity combine in this innocent, worried question. Great economy.

jokes.text: stank-robe-wearing, slipper-shuffling... résumé-polishing, little-kids-don't-like-me blues
jokes.setup: Will's motivational speech to Carlton
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific and rhythmic catalog of Carlton's pathetic state
jokes.explanation: Excellent rhythm and escalation. Each descriptor gets more specific and pathetic, building to a crescendo of Carlton's sad state.

jokes.text: You know, they give you the same amount whether you get six or 16.
jokes.setup: Will returning with chicken nuggets
jokes.punchline: His street-smart observation about sauce packet ratios
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific observation that shows Will's practical street knowledge and mild outrage at unfairness.

jokes.text: Couple more years of seasoning, you know, put a little barbecue sauce on her... let her marinate, you know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Will commenting on Ashley's young friend
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate food metaphor for waiting until she's older
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor that plays on Will's occasional inappropriateness. The food metaphor is creative but the subject matter dates poorly.

jokes.text: P.S. My head is too small for my body.
jokes.setup: Carlton's suicide note listing his problems
jokes.punchline: Adding the head size concern as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Carlton took Will's mockery to heart. The P.S. format adds to the absurdity of prioritizing vanity.

jokes.text: [Carlton singing blues about nobody loving him]
jokes.setup: Carlton in full depression mode
jokes.punchline: His attempt at singing the blues in his preppy voice and style
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Carlton attempting to authentically express pain through a musical form completely foreign to his experience.

jokes.text: Mind? Ha! They practically write out the guest list.
jokes.setup: Kyle asking if Ashley's parents mind her having parties
jokes.punchline: Ashley's exaggerated claim about parental involvement in her rebellion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ashley trying to seem cooler than she is by exaggerating her parents' permissiveness.

jokes.text: Wow, I wish my parents were that cool.
jokes.setup: Ashley claims her parents encourage her parties
jokes.punchline: Kyle believing the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony where audience knows what character doesn't. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Don't worry, he's part of the cleaning crew.
jokes.setup: Kyle noticing Will calling Ashley
jokes.punchline: Ashley's mortifying dismissal of her cousin as hired help
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Ashley prioritizes her image over family loyalty. The casual cruelty is very teenager-like.

jokes.text: How's my hair? How's my face? How's my stuff and my other stuff?
jokes.setup: Ashley's vanity questions to Will
jokes.punchline: Will's mocking repetition with absurd additions
jokes.explanation: Will's exaggerated mimicry of teenage girl behavior. Decent character interaction.

jokes.text: Yeah, we get a runaway yuppie down here every week.
jokes.setup: Will asking about picking up Carlton
jokes.punchline: The bar worker's matter-of-fact classification of Carlton as a 'runaway yuppie'
jokes.explanation: Great original phrase that perfectly captures Carlton's type. The casualness suggests this really is a regular occurrence.

jokes.text: He's over there at the bar throwing back ginger ales.
jokes.setup: Carlton at a bar during his rebellion
jokes.punchline: His 'hard drinking' is actually soda
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of expectations - Carlton's rebellion is still wholesome. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: He's over there at the bar throwing back ginger ales.
jokes.setup: Locating the runaway at the bar
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Carlton's 'hard drinking' involves ginger ale
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific subversion. Sets up expectation of hard drinking then perfectly undercuts it with Carlton's wholesome nature.

jokes.text: He's been telling me about riding the rails. You hear that, Will? Riding the rails. I mean, I don't have a clue what he's talking about... but darn if he doesn't make it sound exciting.
jokes.setup: Carlton bonding with old-timers at the bar
jokes.punchline: His fascination with Depression-era slang he doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his earnest enthusiasm for things he doesn't understand, combined with his sheltered background.

jokes.text: You don't know the blues. How could you?
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming Will doesn't understand hardship
jokes.punchline: The irony of privileged Carlton lecturing street-raised Will about struggle
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Will's response. The irony is clear to the audience even if not to Carlton.

jokes.text: [Will's improvised blues about growing up in the projects]
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming Will doesn't know hardship
jokes.punchline: Will's authentic blues performance highlighting their different backgrounds
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic moment that becomes comedic through contrast with Carlton's earlier attempt. Will's authenticity vs Carlton's affectation is perfectly highlighted.

jokes.text: Excuse me, Pappy, why don't you take a nappy?
jokes.setup: Old man interrupting Will's serious moment
jokes.punchline: Rhyming dismissal that maintains the moment's gravity
jokes.explanation: Clever rhyme that handles interruption without breaking dramatic tension. Good wordplay and timing.

jokes.text: We're gonna have to get you some school supplies. Bye, Ashley.
jokes.setup: Ashley trying to get Kyle's phone number
jokes.punchline: Kyle's dismissive suggestion she needs school supplies (implying she's too young)
jokes.explanation: Clean burn that puts Ashley in her place. The school supplies line is a polite but cutting way to highlight the age gap.

jokes.text: He's even bigger now. Ha-ha-ha. - Yeah, well, aren't we all?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Uncle Phil being big man on campus
jokes.punchline: Awkward exchange about weight gain over time
jokes.explanation: Mild awkward humor about aging and weight. Functional but not particularly memorable or clever.

jokes.text: If you like Eastern Europe in the fall... you should see it in the spring, it's beautiful.
jokes.setup: Interview going well with Carlton's essay about Eastern Europe
jokes.punchline: Carlton's completely irrelevant tourism comment about a geopolitical topic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's tendency to miss the point and make inappropriate small talk during serious moments.

jokes.text: If you like Eastern Europe in the fall... you should see it in the spring, it's beautiful.
jokes.setup: Interviewer mentions Carlton's essay on fall of Eastern Europe
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks he means the season, not political collapse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misunderstanding that reveals Carlton's obliviousness while being perfectly plausible. Excellent craft and impact.

jokes.text: The second one.
jokes.setup: Being asked which charity meant the most to him
jokes.punchline: Carlton's vague, non-committal answer showing he doesn't really care
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in showing Carlton's shallow approach to charity work. The vagueness reveals his insincerity.

jokes.text: Look, friend, we can talk about candy all day... but you ain't gonna convince me that blue was a bad way to go.
jokes.setup: Will taking over Carlton's identity
jokes.punchline: Continuing Carlton's M&M obsession in the interview context
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows Will has absorbed Carlton's bizarre priorities. The casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Listen, Carlton, it's one thing to throw in the hand... but it's another thing to get up from the table and quit the game.
jokes.setup: Carlton ready to give up on life
jokes.punchline: Gruff bar wisdom from an unlikely source
jokes.explanation: Good use of gambling metaphor for life advice. The contrast between the wise words and the seedy setting works well.

jokes.text: You honestly believe that Superman could beat the Flash in a foot race?
jokes.setup: Serious interview moment
jokes.punchline: Will's complete derailment into comic book debate
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's inability to maintain Carlton's facade when discussing topics he actually cares about.

jokes.text: What? That's poppycock. I'm Carlton Banks. No, Will, your charade is not necessary.
jokes.setup: Will has been impersonating Carlton for the interview
jokes.punchline: Carlton's formal language and dramatic entrance
jokes.explanation: Carlton's return with characteristic formality. 'Poppycock' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Like that milquetoast cousin of yours.
jokes.setup: Mr. Bradley praising Will (as Carlton)
jokes.punchline: His dismissal of the real Carlton as weak and boring
jokes.explanation: Good irony as the interviewer prefers Will's authenticity over Carlton's affected persona, not knowing he's got them backwards.

jokes.text: I'm starting to get a little too comfortable in this stuff, know what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Will wearing Carlton's preppy clothes
jokes.punchline: Worried about enjoying the preppy lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Will's self-awareness about class transformation. Good character development moment.
